V-M-036.checks: User frontend must become compact, phone-friendly, and focused on VPN config, subscription, device workflows, and Phase-45 pending trial countdown/calendar UX.
V-M-036.test-files: frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx frontend/src/components/Loading.tsx frontend/src/components/SubscriptionPanel.tsx frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx frontend/src/pages/Config.tsx frontend/src/pages/Subscription.tsx frontend/src/pages/Referrals.tsx frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx frontend/src/index.css scripts/phase45-trial-subscription-ux-smoke.mjs scripts/phase46-user-cabinet-ux-smoke.mjs scripts/phase53-user-matrix-ui-smoke.mjs scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs scripts/phase69-referral-bonus-smoke.mjs scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs scripts/phase72-user-auth-navigation-smoke.mjs scripts/phase73-config-amnezia-qr-smoke.mjs
V-M-036.commands: pass-local 2026-06-05: Phase-73 smoke passed for mobile config KPN copy, AmneziaVPN .conf guidance, tariff-aware limit copy, AmneziaWG QR preservation, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard.

levels.module-local: TypeScript/Vite build must pass for the user frontend after each execution step that changes Phase-23 files.
levels.wave-level: Authenticated mobile route smoke must cover dashboard, config/device, and subscription workflows after UI shell and page changes are merged.
levels.phase-level: Final Phase-23 evidence must record viewport results, config action reachability, and unchanged backend API shapes.

verification-matrix.route-home: Check VPN status, subscription state, primary config CTA, and active device summary appear on first mobile screen or after one navigation tap.
verification-matrix.route-config: Check create device, rotate device, revoke device, QR, copy, and .conf download controls remain present without wide table or raw-config page overflow.
verification-matrix.route-subscription: Check current subscription state, days left, plan price, duration, and renewal/payment button remain readable on all mobile viewports.
verification-matrix.route-phase45-subscription: Check pending-first-VPN state, server-derived days/hours/minutes, and active subscription date calendar remain compact and do not show stale 3-day trial copy.
verification-matrix.route-empty-states: Check loading, forbidden/no-config, no-devices, no-plans, expired-subscription, and request-error states keep an actionable next step.

module-checks.check-2: Mobile dashboard, config, devices, and subscription paths have no document-level horizontal scroll or overlapping text.
module-checks.check-3: Config download/QR action is reachable within one or two taps.
module-checks.check-4: Subscription and device state remain visible without oversized decorative sections.
module-checks.check-5: Create device, rotate device, revoke device, QR, copy, and .conf download controls remain present.
module-checks.check-6: Existing backend API request/response shapes are unchanged in Phase-23.
module-checks.check-7: Phase-45 dashboard and subscription pages render pending activation, remaining days/hours/minutes, and calendar marker from the subscription API fields.
module-checks.check-8: Phase-46 MTProto dashboard card keeps dual-link actions, Russian labels, and bounded status refresh compact in the mobile cabinet.
module-checks.check-9: Phase-53 Matrix redesign keeps dashboard, config, subscription, referrals, and settings compact without decorative route bloat or nested cards.
module-checks.check-10: Phase-53 route smoke confirms primary VPN/config/MTProto/subscription actions are reachable within one or two taps on phone viewports.

phase-53-matrix-route-gates.gate-1: Dashboard first screen prioritizes VPN state, primary config action, MTProto proxy state, subscription countdown, and device slots over decorative content.
phase-53-matrix-route-gates.gate-2: Config route keeps QR, copy, .conf download, create, rotate, and revoke controls compact and unchanged at the API layer.
phase-53-matrix-route-gates.gate-3: Subscription route keeps countdown, calendar, current plan, and tariff CTA readable with stable dimensions.
phase-53-matrix-route-gates.gate-4: Referrals and settings routes use compact rows and safe wrapping for long emails, referral links, device names, and status labels.
phase-53-matrix-route-gates.gate-5: Loading, empty, success, warning, and error states do not resize fixed controls or hide primary actions.

scenarios.scenario-1: Authenticated user opens the cabinet on a phone and immediately sees VPN status, config action, subscription state, and active device summary.
scenarios.scenario-2: User opens config flow, selects or creates a device, then reaches QR, copy, and .conf download without desktop-only layout.
scenarios.scenario-3: User with expired or missing subscription sees a clear renewal/payment action instead of dead config controls.
scenarios.scenario-4: Primary VPN action is buried behind desktop-only navigation or a large decorative layout.
scenarios.scenario-5: A mobile viewport introduces horizontal scroll, hidden controls, clipped critical text, or overlapping action buttons.
scenarios.scenario-6: Simplification removes device-bound rotate/revoke/config retrieval behavior.
scenarios.scenario-7: Pending trial user sees that the four-day timer starts after first VPN connection; activated trial user sees remaining days, hours, minutes, and highlighted active dates.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: No backend API endpoint, request body, or response shape changes are introduced by Phase-23.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Destructive device actions remain visually secondary and confirmation-safe when implemented.
critical-invariants.invariant-3: Expired or missing subscription never leaves the user without a visible payment/renewal path.
critical-invariants.invariant-4: Deploy, install, Docker, nginx, and remote-server automation files remain out of scope.

viewport-gates.viewport-1: Home, config, subscription, and device controls visible without horizontal scroll.
viewport-gates.viewport-2: Primary VPN config path is reachable within one or two taps.
viewport-gates.viewport-3: Long user email, device name, device key, plan name, and status labels do not overlap controls.

observability.telemetry-1: Viewport smoke evidence must include route, viewport, scrollWidth, innerWidth, and checked primary action.
observability.telemetry-2: Evidence must not include access tokens, refresh tokens, raw VPN private keys, raw .env values, or full config contents.
observability.telemetry-3: When browser automation is unavailable, manual evidence must still record the same route and viewport fields.

evidence.evidence-2: Record viewport smoke result with scrollWidth not greater than innerWidth for the tested routes.
evidence.evidence-3: Record action reachability for config QR/download/copy from mobile home.
evidence.evidence-4: Record that device create/rotate/revoke controls and subscription payment entry are still present.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-10: npm run build passed; Chrome CDP smoke passed for /, /config, /subscription at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932 with no horizontal scroll and required actions present.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-06-01: frontend npm run build passed and node scripts/phase45-trial-subscription-ux-smoke.mjs passed for pending trial copy, countdown fields, active-date calendar marker, and stale 3-day copy guard.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-06-01: frontend npm run build passed and node scripts/phase46-user-cabinet-ux-smoke.mjs passed for password example, MTProto dual links, Russian labels, and bounded status refresh.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-06-03: frontend npm run build and node scripts/phase53-user-matrix-ui-smoke.mjs passed for Matrix dashboard/config/subscription/referrals/settings route structure, primary action reachability, state surfaces, and protected deploy/install isolation.
evidence.evidence-9: 2026-06-04: Phase-68 frontend build and dashboard/subscription smoke passed after /dashboard became the compact MTProto-first cabinet with subscription, tariff, and calendar controls directly below it.

V-M-036.autonomous-execution-safety: Autonomous execution may proceed inside Phase-23 user frontend files if each step preserves module contracts, keeps deploy/install out of scope, and records build plus viewport evidence before commit.

phase-68-verification-design.scope: User cabinet layout verification for the merged dashboard/subscription flow.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-1: Sidebar/top logo must use the unframed visual treatment and must not crowd mobile navigation.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-2: Primary dashboard content order must be MTProto first, subscription second, without decorative command-center or secondary-fold bloat.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-3: All cabinet buttons must keep compact Matrix microinteractions, visible focus, disabled states, and reduced-motion fallback.
phase-68-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase46-user-cabinet-ux-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs

phase-69-verification-design.scope: Referral UI verification for masked invite identity and truthful bonus state.
phase-69-verification-design.gate-1: Recent invite rows must show partially masked referred email instead of generic "Реферал #N".
phase-69-verification-design.gate-2: Referral UI must not reveal full referred email, payment IDs, backend user IDs, or hidden bonus internals.
phase-69-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase69-referral-bonus-smoke.mjs

V-M-036.phase-69-evidence: 2026-06-04: frontend build and Phase-69 smoke passed; Referrals rows now render masked referred identity and keep compact status badges.

phase-70-verification-design.scope: Config page compactness and QR reachability verification.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-1: Config page must remove diagnostic/raw-config panels while preserving QR, download, copy, and device configuration workflows.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-2: Mobile config route must have no horizontal scroll and no clipped QR/action controls.
phase-70-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs

V-M-036.phase-70-evidence: 2026-06-04: frontend build and Phase-70/57/62 smokes passed; QR/download/copy/device workflows remain compact and reachable while frontend QR settings are lighter.

phase-71-verification-design.scope: Mobile and tablet master-detail config workflow verification.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-1: Device list, selected-device detail, QR/.conf/Copy sticky actions, and create-disabled-at-limit state must fit 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, and tablet viewports.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-2: Bottom navigation and sticky action controls must not overlap QR, device rows, form fields, or destructive confirmation controls.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-3: Users with 9 devices must not be forced through long uncontrolled scrolling to reach the selected-device export actions.
phase-71-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs

V-M-036.phase-71-evidence: Frontend build and Phase-71 smoke passed for compact device master-detail, mobile sticky QR/.conf/Copy actions, QR close icon, user-shell logo frame removal, and controlled user-cabinet labels.

phase-72-verification-design.scope: Mobile user-cabinet navigation and desktop logout ordering verification.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-1: Mobile bottom navigation must expose Главная, Конфигурация, Подписка, Рефералы, and Настройки at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, and tablet widths.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-2: Bottom navigation must reserve safe-area and content padding so it does not cover QR, download, copy, subscription, tariff, referral, or settings controls.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-3: Desktop sidebar must place "Выйти" directly under "Настройки" and keep logout visible without scrolling the sidebar.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-4: Touch/scroll reveal behavior must not hijack scrolling, intercept pointer events, or hide primary actions.
phase-72-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase72-user-auth-navigation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs

V-M-036.phase-72-evidence: Frontend build passed; Phase-72 smoke passed for the bottom user dock, safe-area spacing, touch reveal marker, and visible desktop logout under Settings; Phase-61 responsive smoke remained green.

phase-73-verification-design.scope: Mobile config route verification for truthful QR/import guidance and tariff-aware device-limit messaging.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-1: /dashboard/config must show "KPN Конфигурация", "Новый конфиг для вашего устройства", and must not render the removed selected-device helper sentence.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-2: AmneziaVPN users must see concise .conf download/import guidance; a non-working AmneziaVPN QR must not be a visible primary scan action.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-3: AmneziaWG QR, selected-device .conf download, and copy actions remain reachable on 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, and tablet widths without bottom-dock overlap.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-4: When device limit is exhausted, the message must say "Лимит устройств исчерпан согласно вашему Тарифу - [тариф]" and must fit compactly on mobile.
phase-73-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase73-config-amnezia-qr-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs

phase-73-evidence.summary: Mobile config workflow keeps selected-device QR/.conf/Copy reachable, no unsupported AmneziaVPN QR tab remains, and the exhausted-device message includes the display tariff name.

phase-76-verification-design.scope: Mobile user cabinet regression verification across Phase-72 and Phase-73.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-1: Phone and tablet routes must keep dashboard, config, subscription, referrals, settings, bottom dock, QR, .conf, copy, and tariff-limit messaging reachable without overlap.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-2: Phase-61 responsive smoke must remain part of the final Phase-76 bundle.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-3: Phase-45, Phase-68, Phase-70, and Phase-71 smokes must prove trial, calendar, config, and master-detail behavior did not regress.
phase-76-verification-design.commands: node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs; Phase-72/73 smokes.

V-M-036.phase-76-evidence: Consolidated Phase-76 gate passed for mobile user cabinet: Phase-72/73 smokes plus Phase-57/61/68/70/71 regressions kept bottom dock, selected-device config, subscription calendar, and Amnezia guidance reachable without backend/deploy/runtime drift.

phase-77-verification-design.scope: Mobile user-cabinet verification for always-visible bottom navigation and touch-scroll stability.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-1: Mobile bottom navigation must remain fixed at the bottom of the viewport with safe-area padding.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-2: Main user content must reserve enough bottom padding and avoid mobile overflow-hidden clipping.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-3: Matrix visual layers and motion markers must not intercept mobile touch scroll.
phase-77-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase77-subscription-mobile-polish-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase72-user-auth-navigation-smoke.mjs.

V-M-036.phase-77-evidence: Phase-77 smoke, Phase-61 responsive smoke, and Phase-72 user navigation smoke passed for fixed bottom dock, safe-area content padding, scroll-surface markers, and no protected backend/deploy/runtime/admin drift.

config-action-polish-verification.gate-1: QR, .conf, and "Копировать конфиг" must remain reachable from the selected-device detail without horizontal scroll.
config-action-polish-verification.gate-2: The copy button must use nowrap text and narrow-phone sizing so "Копировать" does not split across lines.
config-action-polish-verification.gate-3: Bottom dock, sticky actions, and safe-area padding must remain compatible with the Phase-72/77 mobile navigation contract.
config-action-polish-verification.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase72-user-auth-navigation-smoke.mjs.

V-M-036.config-action-polish-evidence: Phase-71 smoke asserts [MobileUserCabinet][fix][COPY_CONFIG_NO_WRAP] and the protected backend/deploy/runtime guard remains clean.
